_note: Manually maintained. Challenger data cannot be sourced from APIs — update this file directly in the GitHub repo when primary results change.

challengers.name: Brandon Herrera
challengers.note: Firearms YouTuber and gunsmith; ran vs. pro-Israel Rep. Tony Gonzales on opposition to open-ended foreign military aid. Forced runoff Mar 3.

challengers.name: James Talarico
challengers.note: TX state Rep. Won Democratic Senate primary Mar 3, 2026. Pledged to ban offensive arms sales to Israel. Faces Cornyn or Paxton in November.

challengers.name: Nida Allam
challengers.note: Durham Co. Commissioner; endorsed by Bernie Sanders. Foushee leads 49.18% vs 48.22% — margin of 1,202 votes. Allam confirmed recount request. $4.5M outside spending set NC primary record.

challengers.name: Frederick Haynes III
challengers.note: Won Mar 3 primary with ~74% of the vote. Dallas pastor; endorsed by Crockett; calls for ceasefire and end to offensive US arms to Israel. Heavily favored in general (D+25 district).

challengers.name: Zeeshan Hafeez
challengers.note: Georgetown Law/UVA MBA; no corporate PAC money; calls for end to US arms sales to Israel. Running in 4-way primary vs. incumbent Julie Johnson and former Rep. Colin Allred. Results pending Mar 3.

challengers.name: Kat Abughazaleh
challengers.note: Journalist and digital creator; grandchild of Nakba survivors; endorsed by CAIR Action and Justice Democrats. Calls for full arms embargo. Running for Schakowsky's open IL-9 seat (3-way with Biss and Fine).

challengers.name: Daniel Biss
challengers.note: Evanston mayor; endorsed by Schakowsky; supports ceasefire and weapons pause to Israel. Running for same IL-9 open seat (3-way with Abughazaleh and Fine). Leads in early polls at ~24%.

challengers.name: Junaid Ahmed
challengers.note: Arms embargo advocate for open seat. Endorsed by Khanna, Ramirez, Jayapal. Led fundraising at $838K.

challengers.name: Abdul el-Sayed
challengers.note: Former Oakland Co. exec; calls the Gaza campaign genocide; opposes all offensive US arms. Vs. Rep. Haley Stevens.

challengers.name: Cori Bush
challengers.note: Defeated Aug 2024 — AIPAC spent $8.5M. 2026 comeback filed vs. Wesley Bell. Endorsed by DSA and Tlaib.

challengers.name: Summer Shelton
challengers.note: Progressive challenger on full arms embargo vs. Rep. Lucy McBath.

challengers.name: Jack Schlossberg
challengers.note: Running for MA Senate 2026. Pro-Palestinian lean; opposes offensive arms transfers.
